own_review_points: SpannerPostgreSQLDialect.getCheckCondition(String,String[]) does NOT escape single quotes in values (appends '+value+'), unlike the Collection overload that does value.replace("'","''") -> correctness/injection risk
Calendar overload of appendDateTimeLiteral lacks @Override while sibling TemporalAccessor/Date overloads have it
SpannerConcatFunction declares unused private constant CAST="::text" then hardcodes the literal (dead code)
renderDmlTargetTableExpression deviates from super (always renders alias) but is intentional per comment (Spanner has no FROM in UPDATE)
SPANNER_LOCKING_SUPPORT=NO_LOCKING_SUPPORT yet getForUpdateString() still returns ' for update' (TODO acknowledges)
renderLikePattern emulation in SpannerPostgreSQLSqlAstTranslator implements the nested replace() the human reference suggested for like-escape
docker_db.sh adds curl-based gcloud install -> CI network/time cost
Widespread @Entity(name) workarounds for '$' across ~30 test files instead of a naming strategy
notes: 124-file PR, diff truncated at 150k (53k more unseen). Cited code is in the visible window. Point 7 is WRONG: it claims all three getCheckCondition overrides escape via value.replace("'","''"), but the String[] overload does NO escaping at all -> mischaracterizes the code AND misses the real unescaped-value bug (counted wrong + the bug counted as missed). Point 3 (scalar subquery) is sound-design restating with a 'verify' ask -> trivial. Points 1,2,4,5,6,8,9 are correct/grounded. Missed the human-ref DialectFeatureChecks:1419 emulation note (the PR did implement it) and the String[] escaping bug. Solid but one factual slip.
